Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable for all relocations assigned to Suki Patel using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name "Assigned Coordinator", and value "Suki" (or similar). Expected findings: 16 total relocations assigned to Suki. 8 are "In Progress": Daniel Reeves (Vectral, Seattle to San Diego), Maya Johansson (Vectral, Portland to San Diego), Simone Richter (BrightLoop, Chicago to Boston), Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, Atlanta to Boston), Camille Oduya (Vectral, Boston to Seattle), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic, Tampa to Denver), Andre Mitchell (Mosaic, Columbus to Boise), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, Austin to SF). Also marked Completed: Jae-won Kim (StormCloud, Seattle to Austin) — incorrectly, as the lease at Barton Creek Flats remains unsigned — and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, San Diego to Boston) with in-person apartment visit requirement and unconfirmed permanent housing.
OE 2: Search emails and Slack using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "visit apartments", "apartment in person", or "Ekwueme" (or similar) to find the employee who wanted in-person apartment visits. Expected findings: Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop Analytics, San Diego to Boston). Temp housing at The Hive in Kendall Square (April 22-25, $780). UrbanNest (Carmen Reyes) sent 3 permanent apartment options: Cambridge/Central Square ($2,700/mo), Somerville/Davis Square ($2,400/mo), Brookline ($2,550/mo).
OE 3: Search emails and Slack using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Ekwueme apartment", "Ekwueme lease", or "Jordan apartment" (or similar) to confirm whether Jordan Ekwueme selected a permanent apartment. Expected finding: No confirmation found. No email or Slack record exists showing Jordan selected a permanent apartment from the 3 UrbanNest options. Temp housing was ending around April 25. Permanent housing status is unknown despite the relocation record being marked "Completed."
OE 4: Search emails and Slack using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to employee names, vendor names, "Swift", "Road Runner", or "vendor confirmation" (or similar) to find vendor confirmations across all In Progress relocations. Expected findings: Terrence Nakamura has no Swift Relocations confirmation for Shipment 2 (server rack + books, ~3,200 lbs); Swift issued a 15% rate increase and $500 DOT surcharge effective April 11 with capacity strained across 3-4 concurrent MoveOps jobs in the April 15-27 window. Marcus Webb (BrightLoop) vehicle shipping via Road Runner Auto Transport (~$1,100) still pending; Road Runner pushed delivery from April 8 to April 18-20 window and Blessing missed the dispatch update. Deshawn Hartley and Andre Mitchell both have no vendor confirmation on file.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to employee names combined with "housing", "apartment", "lease", or "guarantor" (or similar) to find housing status across all In Progress relocations. Expected findings: Jae-won Kim's lease at Barton Creek Flats (Austin) is unsigned - UrbanNest (Carmen Reyes) requested MoveOps corporate guarantor packet on April 8 and it was never sent; Jae-won emailed support@moveops.com on April 15 asking for lease docs (unread); employee still in temp housing (~18+ days). Terrence Nakamura has no ADA-accessible housing confirmed in SF (needs roll-in shower, 36-inch doorways, ground floor or elevator, SoMa or Mission Bay). Simone Richter emailed Mina reporting she was placed in a studio instead of the 1BR that was communicated; email is unread; she has been there ~3 weeks. Deshawn Hartley has no housing confirmation on file (needed furnished 1BR near Denver Convention Center). Andre Mitchell has no housing confirmation on file (needs garage/workspace apartment in Boise).
OE 6: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Chloe" (or similar). Expected finding: Chloe Vance, Operations Manager, email chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 7: Find the #operations Slack channel using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel". Expected finding: #operations channel ID is C006.
OE 8: Update Jae-won Kim's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) to flag the unsigned lease at Barton Creek Flats, the missing MoveOps corporate guarantor packet that was never sent to UrbanNest, and correct the status.
OE 9: Update Jordan Ekwueme's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recReloc00000008) to flag the unconfirmed permanent apartment selection and that housing status is unknown despite temp housing ending around April 25.
OE 10: Update Terrence Nakamura's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF) to flag the unconfirmed Swift Relocations booking for the specialty shipment (server rack requiring padded crating and climate-controlled transport) and the unconfirmed ADA-accessible housing in San Francisco.
OE 11: Update Marcus Webb's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recMarcusWebbBrightloop) to flag the unresolved vehicle shipping status with Road Runner Auto Transport and the delivery delay (revised window April 18-20).
OE 12: Update Deshawn Hartley's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver) to flag the missing vendor confirmation and missing housing confirmation (needed furnished 1BR near Denver Convention Center for Automate 2026 trade show).
OE 13: Update Andre Mitchell's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recReloc00000000038) to flag the missing vendor confirmation and missing housing confirmation (needs garage/workspace apartment in Boise for mechanical prototyping).
OE 14: Send a message to Chloe in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006, user_id: moveops_suki_patel) covering the critical issues found: the Jae-won Kim lease/guarantor issue flagged as urgent, Terrence Nakamura's unconfirmed Swift vendor booking and unconfirmed ADA-accessible housing, the Simone Richter housing mismatch (studio instead of promised 1BR), and the Deshawn Hartley and Andre Mitchell missing vendor and housing confirmations.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
